name: Beat Matching
description: A mechanic where player inputs are evaluated against musical beat positions using an audio clock, chart data, and latency-adjusted judgment windows.
design_notes: Beat matching depends on trust in the timing model. The player can forgive difficulty, but not inconsistent judgment. The design should define the authoritative audio clock, beat index conversion, latency calibration, pause behavior, dropped-frame tolerance, and how generous each judgment band should be.
design_purpose: Make rhythmic precision the central expression of skill.
Connect music structure to input, scoring, enemy actions, or movement.
Give designers measurable timing bands for difficulty, assists, and score tiers.
player_fantasy: The player feels locked into the music when their inputs land exactly on the intended beat.

parameters.name: song_id
parameters.description: Audio track associated with the beat chart and playback clock.

parameters.name: chart_id
parameters.description: Chart definition that maps beats to expected player actions.

parameters.name: beat_index
parameters.description: Beat number currently evaluated by chart and input logic.

parameters.name: beat_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Audio-clock timestamp for the expected beat location.

parameters.name: bpm
parameters.description: Tempo used when the chart does not require a full tempo map.

parameters.name: input_event_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Input timestamp captured before render-frame delays affect judgment.

parameters.name: calibration_profile_id
parameters.description: Latency offset profile applied before scoring the input.

edge_cases: The audio device reports a playhead jump after pause, buffering, or output-device change.
A player input arrives between two valid beats and could be consumed by either note.
The game drops render frames while audio playback continues correctly.
A tempo map changes bpm mid-song and beat_index conversion needs to remain stable.
Calibration is changed during a song and should not alter already judged inputs.
common_bugs: Input is judged against frame delta instead of audio_playhead_ms, causing drift under load.
Latency offset is applied twice to held notes or repeated taps.
Pause resumes chart scrolling but not the audio clock baseline.
Floating-point beat accumulation creates different judgments across long tracks.
Replay data stores visual note positions instead of input timestamps and cannot reproduce scores.
balancing_notes: Tighter windows increase mastery but magnify device latency and accessibility barriers.
Early and late feedback should be symmetrical unless the music or control scheme has a deliberate bias.
Assist modes can widen judgment windows without changing chart identity if scores mark assisted play clearly.
accessibility_notes: Provide audio latency calibration, visual latency calibration, and per-player offset profiles.
Offer wider timing windows or practice modes for players with motor or device-latency constraints.
Use visual beat markers and haptic cues as alternatives to audio-only timing.

implementation_notes.unity: Use AudioSettings.dspTime or an equivalent audio clock as the timing authority, then convert chart beat positions into milliseconds using bpm or tempo_map_id. Capture input timestamps as close to the input event as possible and apply calibration_profile_id once during judgment.
implementation_notes.godot: Use AudioServer playback position plus output latency compensation for audio_playhead_ms. Keep chart_id data separate from rendered notes, and judge inputs from timestamped events rather than node positions on screen.
implementation_notes.unreal: Quartz clocks or audio component playback data can own beat scheduling. Route input_event_timestamp_ms through a rhythm subsystem, apply calibration offsets before hit judgment, and keep scoring deterministic for replay verification.
implementation_notes.web: Use Web Audio currentTime as the authoritative playhead and DOM or pointer event timestamps for input. Rendered animation should chase chart state, while scoring reads audio_playhead_ms and calibration offsets from data.

example_games.title: Beat Saber
example_games.note: Evaluates player actions against musical timing and charted targets.

example_games.title: Crypt of the NecroDancer
example_games.note: Connects movement and combat actions to musical beats.
